question: I'm researching a Texas county that ranked in the top five for average summer meal program sites from 2018-19, ... 2022-2023. In 2022, it also had under 12% of its population aged 65+ and more than 3,000 children served in family preservation services. Who were the two counties named after? Please only report the absolute difference in their birth years in your final answer. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
practical_motivation: A public health researcher studying childhood nutrition access wants to identify which major Texas counties maintain robust summer meal programs while also having younger demographic profiles and significant child welfare caseloads. Understanding the historical relationship between county and city namesakes provides insight into early Texas settlement patterns and the distinct contributions of different founders to Texas history.

1.fact: In 2018-2019, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1052), DALLAS (1014), HIDALGO (494), BEXAR (411), TARRANT (367).
1.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2018-2019? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2018-2019'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

2.fact: In 2019-2020, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1919), DALLAS (1479), BEXAR (1038), TARRANT (688), HIDALGO (515).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2019-2020? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2019-2020'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

3.fact: In 2020-2021, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (2674), DALLAS (1504), BEXAR (1168), TARRANT (1033), HIDALGO (700).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2020-2021? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2020-2021'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

4.fact: In 2021-2022, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1825), DALLAS (1545), BEXAR (814), TARRANT (757), HIDALGO (526).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2021-2022? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2021-2022'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

5.fact: In 2022-2023, the Texas counties with the most summer meal program sites were: HARRIS (1016), DALLAS (688), BEXAR (364), HIDALGO (353), TARRANT (303).
5.subquestion: Which Texas counties had the most summer meal program sites in 2022-2023? (Filter: ProgramYear == '2022-2023'; count sites by SiteCounty; take top 5.)

6.fact: In 2022, among the top 5 counties by average summer meal sites, Harris (11.2%) and Dallas (11.5%) had less than 12% of their population aged 65 and older, while Bexar (13.1%), Tarrant (13.1%), and Hidalgo (12.3%) had 12% or more.
6.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 1-5 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Tarrant, Hidalgo> had less than 12% of their population aged 65 and older in 2022? (Filter: Fiscal Year == 2022; use '65 and older: % of Population' < 12.)

7.fact: In 2022, among the top 5 counties by average summer meal sites, Harris (3312), Bexar (4612), and Tarrant (3101) had more than 3000 children served in family preservation services, while Dallas (2582) and Hidalgo (882) had fewer.
7.subquestion: Which counties from <nodes 1-5 top 5 by average: Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Tarrant, Hidalgo> had more than 3000 children served in family preservation services in 2022? (Filter: Fiscal Year == 2022; sum 'Children served in FBSS Family Preservation Services' by County across race/ethnicity; keep > 3000.)

8.fact: According to Wikipedia, Harris County was founded on December 22, 1836 and 'is named for John Richardson Harris, who founded the town of Harrisburg on Buffalo Bayou in 1826.' Its county seat is Houston.
8.subquestion: Who is <intersection of nodes 6-7: Harris County> named after, and what is its county seat?

9.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'The city is named after former General Sam Houston, who was president of the Republic of Texas and had won Texas's independence from Mexico at the Battle of San Jacinto.'
9.subquestion: Who is <node 8 county seat: Houston> named after?

10.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'John Richardson Harris (October 22, 1790 – August 21, 1829) was an American settler of Mexican Texas and the namesake of Harris County, Texas.'
10.subquestion: In what year was <node 8: John Richardson Harris> born?

11.fact: According to Wikipedia, 'Samuel Houston (March 2, 1793 – July 26, 1863) was an American general and statesman who played a prominent role in the Texas Revolution.'
11.subquestion: In what year was <node 9: Sam Houston> born?

reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=5, data.gov AGGREGATION - nodes 1-5):
Node 1: Summer Meal 2018-2019 → top 5 counties by site count (see node 1 answer)
Node 2: Summer Meal 2019-2020 → top 5 counties by site count (see node 2 answer)
Node 3: Summer Meal 2020-2021 → top 5 counties by site count (see node 3 answer)
Node 4: Summer Meal 2021-2022 → top 5 counties by site count (see node 4 answer)
Node 5: Summer Meal 2022-2023 → top 5 counties by site count (see node 5 answer)
AGGREGATION: Compute average across all 5 years → Top 5 are {HARRIS: 1697.2, DALLAS: 1246.0, BEXAR: 759.0, TARRANT: 629.6, HIDALGO: 517.6}
Note: All 5 years required because Harris leads overall while other counties shift in rank by year
HOP 2 (d=2, data.gov INTERSECTION - nodes 6-7):
Node 6: From top 5, elderly < 12% → {Harris, Dallas}
Node 7: From top 5, family preservation > 3000 → {Harris, Bexar, Tarrant}
INTERSECTION: {Harris, Dallas} ∩ {Harris, Bexar, Tarrant} = {Harris}
Both criteria necessary: Node 6 alone gives 2 counties, Node 7 alone gives 3 counties
HOP 3 (d=2, Wikipedia - nodes 8-9):
Node 8: Harris County Wikipedia → named after John Richardson Harris, county seat is Houston
Node 9: Houston Wikipedia → city named after Sam Houston
Result: Two namesakes identified - John Richardson Harris (county) and Sam Houston (city)
HOP 4 (d=2, Wikipedia AGGREGATION - nodes 10-11):
Node 10: John Richardson Harris Wikipedia → born October 22, 1790
Node 11: Sam Houston Wikipedia → born March 2, 1793
AGGREGATION: 1793 - 1790 = 3 years difference
Final answer: 3
